note: FROZEN pre-registration (#976) — never edit; delete deliberately to regenerate.

predictions.claim_natural: During the first 7 days (Days 1–7), nightly time-in-bed will consistently reach the 7.5-hour target window (9:00 PM to 4:30 AM), with the wearable logging at least 7.0 hours of total sleep on 5 or more of the 7 nights, as the fixed schedule is new and sleep efficiency typically needs several nights to consolidate — direction here reflects whether logged sleep duration trends upward across the 7-night window as the body adapts to the earlier 9:00 PM bedtime.

Over the first 14 days (Days 1–14), wearable-measured HRV will trend upward from its Day 1 baseline value, reflecting progressive parasympathetic adaptation to the structured 9:00 PM–4:30 AM sleep schedule and the plan's ramp-discipline rule of reduced training volume with no failure sets in Week 1 and no PRs before Day 21 — both of which limit acute physiological stress that would otherwise suppress overnight HRV recovery.

Daily calorie intake will remain at or below the 1500 kcal target on at least 5 out of every 7 logged days during the first 14 days, as the structured 11:30am-7:30pm eating window mechanically limits opportunity for excess intake.

Daily protein intake will meet or exceed the 170 g floor on at least 5 out of every 7 logged days during the first 14 days, given that 170 g protein within a 1500 kcal budget represents approximately 45% of calories and requires deliberate food selection that the plan explicitly mandates.

During the first 14 days, daily walking will consistently meet the 3-mile minimum on all 14 days, as the ramp discipline explicitly prescribes reduced volume and no failure sets, making the walking component the primary measurable output of the Foundation phase in week 1 and week 2.

Over the first 14 days, weekly Zone 2 training time will trend upward from a sub-150-min total in week 1 (reduced-volume ramp week) toward the 150 min/week target by the end of week 2, as the plan explicitly holds Zone 2 to RPE-guided effort for the first 2 weeks before any intensity benchmarking.

Adherence to the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window will be maintained on at least 6 out of 7 days during the first 14 days, as the structured window provides a clear behavioral rule that is easiest to follow when motivation is highest at the start of a new plan.

The ramp discipline rule of no failure sets and no PRs before Day 21 will produce at least one logged instance of self-reported restraint or intentional hold-back in the training journal during the first 14 days, reflecting the psychological friction of suppressing competitive effort when energy and novelty are high in Week 1.

Body weight will trend downward from the 315.0 lb start weight over the first 14 days, driven by the 1500 kcal/day caloric target combined with daily ~3-mile walking and 5 gym days/week of Foundation-phase strength training.

Daily protein intake will consistently meet or exceed the 170 g protein floor across the first 14 days, as logged in the nutrition record, reflecting the plan's explicit protein minimum requirement within the 1500 kcal/day target.

Adhering to the 11:30am–7:30pm eating window daily across the first 14 days will produce a consistent, measurable pattern of fasting glucose readings (taken before 11:30am) that trend downward over the window, as the 8-hour time-restricted feeding protocol takes effect alongside the 1500 kcal/day deficit relative to a 315-lb starting body mass.

The combination of 25 g/day minimum fiber intake and at least 3 miles of post-work walking daily will produce a downward trend in post-meal glucose excursions (as logged via CGM or fingerstick 2 hours after the first meal of the eating window) across the first 14 days, with readings becoming progressively lower by day 14 compared to day 1.

Resting heart rate will trend downward over the first 14 days, consistent with the daily walking (~3 miles) and 150 min/week Zone 2 target beginning on Day 1, moving toward the plan's stated RHR target of 55 bpm.

HRV will trend upward over the first 14 days, supported by the structured 7.5-hour sleep target (bed 9:00 PM, wake 4:30 AM), the ramp discipline of reduced volume and no failure sets in week 1, and no PRs before day 21 — all of which limit acute autonomic stress and allow recovery adaptation toward the plan's 50 ms HRV target.

Within the first 14 days, daily calorie log entries will show at least 12 out of 14 days recorded (≥85% adherence rate), establishing a consistent N=1 data-capture baseline before any meaningful physiological signal can be separated from noise.

Across the first 14 days, day-to-day bodyweight variance will be high enough that no monotonic downward trend will be statistically detectable in the raw daily scale readings — given the 315.0 lb start weight and the acute fluid and glycogen shifts expected when a 1500 kcal/day eating window protocol begins, the within-week standard deviation of daily weigh-ins will obscure any true fat-loss signal, making a 7-day rolling average necessary to see directional movement.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days when logged calories are at or under the 1500 kcal plan target, next-day weight will be lower than after days over target.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: 1500 kcal/day target from a 315.0 lb start (config/user_goals.json); MacroFactor logs daily calories and Withings logs daily weight, so both arms are directly measurable.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Mean next-day weight at least 0.1 lbs lower on adherent days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Hold the 1,500 kcal baseline through the Foundation phase; escalate only if the trend stalls.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On days reaching roughly 6,000+ steps (the 3-mile daily-movement floor), next-day Whoop recovery will be higher than after low-movement days.
hypotheses.evidence: Pre-registered at genesis from the plan itself: daily movement target '3 miles walking minimum (steps will vary by stride length, roughly 6,000-7,000 steps)' (config/user_goals.json); Apple Health logs steps and Whoop logs recovery daily.
hypotheses.confirmation_criteria: Next-day recovery at least 3 points higher on 6,000+ step days within 30 days, 95% CI excluding 0.
hypotheses.actionable_if_confirmed: Protect the daily walk as a recovery lever, not just an energy-expenditure line.
